Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

I think we need to use .iterate instead of .readdir for v3.11 kernel and later. .iterate is introduced to replace .readdir in v3.11. There is a small time window within v3.11 development cycle when both of them can work. But .readdir is deleted after all in-kernel users are converted, before v3.11 is released.

Patch Set 3:

Peng, Not sure I understand your comment.  It looks like the changes in dir.c in this patch series does already change over to using .iterate instead of .readdir.

Patch Set 3: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Sorry, I missed. I only read the commit message last time and .iterate is not mentioned. The patch looks good.

commit.subject: LU-3974 llite: use new struct dir_context
commit.message:
LU-3974 llite: use new struct dir_context

The readdir and nfs code over time has added more
parameters to be passed to be processed. For the 3.11
kernel a new struct dir_context was introduced to
minimize the impact of future expansion. This patch
addresses this change.

Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Change-Id: Ib42bf8cb06635a2a64e63b294d79e66ac82a1a5b
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/7747
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Peng Tao <bergwolf@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
